meta.title: Unit 3 — Action Verbs
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Learn to identify and use action verbs in sentences.

questions.question: Which word is an action verb?
questions.explanation: "run" is an action verb — it describes something you do.

questions.question: Which word is an action verb?
questions.explanation: "sing" is an action verb — it is something you can do.

questions.question: "Jump" is an action verb.
questions.explanation: Yes! "Jump" describes an action — something you can do.

questions.question: "Beautiful" is an action verb.
questions.explanation: "Beautiful" is an adjective (a describing word), not a verb.

questions.question: She ___ to school every day.
questions.explanation: "walks" is the action verb — it tells what she does.

questions.question: Yesterday, I ___ (walk) to school.
questions.explanation: walk → walked (regular verb: add -ed for past tense)

questions.question: She ___ (play) with her friends after school.
Sort each word — is it a verb or a noun?
questions.explanation: run/swim/sing are actions (verbs). apple/table are things (nouns).

questions.question: Sort each word — is it a verb or an adjective?
questions.explanation: eat/jump/cook are actions (verbs). red/small describe things (adjectives).

questions.question: Which sentence uses the verb correctly?
questions.explanation: "read" (past tense) is correct here. "yesterday" tells us it happened in the past.

questions.question: In the sentence "The dog eats food," the verb is "dog."
questions.explanation: "dog" is a noun. The verb is "eats" — it tells what the dog does.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "She reads books every day."

questions.question: He ___ (like) ice cream.
questions.explanation: like → likes (add -s when the subject is he, she, or it)

questions.question: Which sentence is in the past tense?
questions.explanation: "ate" is the past tense of "eat." It tells us the action already happened.
